name: Lamivudine
description: A reverse transcriptase inhibitor and zalcitabine analog in which a sulfur atom replaces the 3' carbon of the pentose ring. It is used to treat Human Immunodeficiency Virus Type 1 (HIV-1) and hepatitis B (HBV).
indication: For the treatment of HIV infection and chronic hepatitis B (HBV).
toxicity: The most common reported adverse reactions (incidence ≥15%) in adults were headache, nausea, malaise and fatigue, nasal signs and symptoms, diarrhea, and cough.
mechanismOfAction: Lamivudine is a synthetic nucleoside analogue and is phosphorylated intracellularly to its active 5'-triphosphate metabolite, lamivudine triphosphate (L-TP). This nucleoside analogue is incorporated into viral DNA by HIV reverse transcriptase and HBV polymerase, resulting in DNA chain termination.
pharmacodynamics: Lamivudine is a nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor (NRTI) with activity against Human Immunodeficiency Virus Type 1 (HIV-1) and hepatitis B (HBV). Lamivudine is phosphorylated to active metabolites that compete for incorporation into viral DNA. They inhibit the HIV reverse transcriptase enzyme competitively and act as a chain terminator of DNA synthesis. The lack of a 3'-OH group in the incorporated nucleoside analogue prevents the formation of the 5' to 3' phosphodiester linkage essential for DNA chain elongation, and therefore, the viral DNA growth is terminated.

interactions.name: Emtricitabine
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Lamivudine is combined with Emtricitabine.

interactions.name: Ganciclovir
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Ganciclovir is combined with Lamivudine.

interactions.name: Valganciclovir
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Valganciclovir is combined with Lamivudine.

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Lamivudine can be increased when it is combined with Vemurafenib.

interactions.name: Ribavirin
interactions.description: Ribavirin may increase the hepatotoxic activities of Lamivudine.

interactions.name: Tipiracil
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Tipiracil can be increased when it is combined with Lamivudine.

interactions.name: Ranolazine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Lamivudine can be increased when it is combined with Ranolazine.

interactions.name: Trimethoprim
interactions.description: Trimethoprim may decrease the excretion rate of Lamivudine which could result in a higher serum level.

interactions.name: Bupropion
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Lamivudine can be increased when it is combined with Bupropion.

interactions.name: Teriflunomide
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Lamivudine can be increased when it is combined with Teriflunomide.

interactions.name: Lumacaftor
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Lamivudine can be decreased when it is combined with Lumacaftor.

interactions.name: Rolapitant
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Lamivudine can be increased when it is combined with Rolapitant.

interactions.name: Eltrombopag
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Lamivudine can be increased when it is combined with Eltrombopag.
